entities.name: INDIAN PEANUT CLUMP VIRUS
entities.description: A virus, specifically the Hyderabad isolate, that causes disease in groundnut.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The target crop used for virus transmission studies and resistance screening.

entities.name: MECHANICAL INOCULATION
entities.description: A technique of transmitting a virus using infected leaf sap or purified virus preparations.

entities.name: FRENCH BEAN
entities.description: A crop (Phaseolus vulgaris cv. Topcrop) used as an intermediate host and inoculum source for the virus.

entities.name: VEINAL NECROSIS
entities.description: Symptoms typical of Indian peanut clump virus infection.

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of groundnut germplasm to resist infection by IPCV and PCV isolates.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE CHAMBERS
entities.description: Controlled environments where inoculated plants were maintained.

entities.name: TEMPERATURE
entities.description: Environmental conditions including 27-35°C for growth and -70°C for seed storage.

entities.name: PEANUT CLUMP VIRUS
entities.description: A group of virus isolates, including PCV, used for screening groundnut resistance.
